questionHtml: Outline the role of membrane proteins in the movement of specific ions at specific times in the transmission of nerve impulses.
markschemeHtml:
1.  (during depolarization) Na+ channels open;
  2.  allowing Na+ to flow into the axon;
  3.  (during repolarization) K+ channels open;
  4.  allowing K+ to flow out of the axon;
  5.  the Na+/K+ pump returns the (axon) membrane to the resting potential;
  6.  by moving 3Na+ out and 2K+ in;

meta.title: Outline the role of membrane proteins in the movement of specific ions at specific times in the transmission of nerve impulses.
breadcrumbLabels: C2.2.3. Nerve impulses as action potentials that are propagated along nerve fibres
